title: Syracuse Common Council approves sale of old fire station for $900K renovation project
summary: Syracuse Common Council approved the sale of a vacant East Fayette Street fire station to JF Real Estate for a $900,000 renovation project. The former station, inactive for over a decade, will be converted into four second-floor apartments and a ground-floor restaurant. This represents mixed-use adaptive reuse of city-owned property aligned with economic development and housing goals.

political_signal.description: Council approval of adaptive reuse project signals alignment with downtown revitalization and mixed-use development priorities. Project converts dormant municipal asset into housing + commercial space.

processing_notes: Project timeline incomplete—article notes developers 'have not shared when they hope to have the project completed'
Discrepancy in renovation budget description: headline states '$900K' but article references 'multi-million-dollar renovations'—clarification needed on actual project cost
Location: East Fayette Street, Syracuse (specific address not provided)
No community benefit agreement, affordable housing requirements, or workforce development terms mentioned in article
